content_markdown:
The graph shows the variation of pressure $P$ with volume $V$ for a gas undergoing an adiabatic expansion.

Which of the following areas correctly identifies the work done by the gas?

diagram_metadata.description: A P-V diagram showing an adiabatic expansion curve. The area under the curve is divided into two regions: a shaded upper region labeled X and a dotted lower rectangular region labeled Z extending to the V-axis.

vibe_explanation: In a pressure-volume (P-V) diagram, the work done by a gas during an expansion is represented by the total area under the curve between the initial and final volumes. This area is the integral $\int P dV$. In the provided diagram, the total area under the adiabatic curve consists of both the shaded region X and the dotted region Z, so the total work done is X + Z.
